items.Source_File: Pasted text.txt — Thai allergic rhinitis CPG update 2565 / 2022
items.Recommendation_Extract: Thai AR CPG covers both children and adults and is intended for GP/ENT/pediatric/allergy use.
items.Patch_Use: Use as source basis for AR adult/peds rows.
items.Caveat: Not a product-specific local inventory file.

items.Source_File: Pasted text.txt — Thai allergic rhinitis CPG update 2565 / 2022
items.Recommendation_Extract: Saline irrigation is recommended and useful for adults, children, and pregnancy in allergic rhinitis.
items.Patch_Use: Add Klean & Kare saline as multimodality RX support.

items.Source_File: Pasted text.txt — Thai allergic rhinitis CPG update 2565 / 2022
items.Recommendation_Extract: 2nd-generation oral H1-antihistamines are preferred over 1st-generation antihistamines for nasal symptom control.
items.Patch_Use: Keep Zyrtec/Telfast/Bilaxten as oral antihistamine options.

items.Source_File: Pasted text.txt — Thai allergic rhinitis CPG update 2565 / 2022
items.Recommendation_Extract: Intranasal corticosteroids control nasal symptoms better than oral antihistamines and are recommended first for persistent moderate/severe AR.
items.Caveat: Duration in patch remains clinic default; source verifies dose/role.

items.Source_File: Pasted text.txt — Thai allergic rhinitis CPG update 2565 / 2022
items.Recommendation_Extract: Pediatric cetirizine age-band doses: 6–11 mo 2.5 mg OD; 1–2 y 2.5 mg OD/BID; 2–5 y 5 mg OD; >6 y 5–10 mg OD.
items.Patch_Use: Replace vague Zyrtec syrup 1–2 tsp with age-band rows.

items.Source_File: Pasted text.txt — Thai allergic rhinitis CPG update 2565 / 2022
items.Recommendation_Extract: Pediatric mometasone: age 2–11 y 1 spray/nostril OD; age ≥12 y 2 sprays/nostril OD.
items.Caveat: Use only if age ≥2 y; monitor if prolonged.

items.Source_File: Pasted text.txt — Thai allergic rhinitis CPG update 2565 / 2022
items.Recommendation_Extract: Decongestants are not recommended in children under 2 years.
items.Patch_Use: Add age <2 avoid rule for Iliadin/Nasotapp-style decongestants.
items.Caveat: Short course only even when age appropriate.

items.Source_File: Pasted text (2).txt — Thai epistaxis practice guideline 2567 / 2024
items.Recommendation_Extract: Epistaxis guideline recommends topical decongestant/lidocaine preparation and describes 0.05% oxymetazoline or 0.1% xylometazoline use during management.

items.Source_File: Pasted text (2).txt — Thai epistaxis practice guideline 2567 / 2024
items.Recommendation_Extract: Anterior packing materials may be coated with antibiotic ointment such as terramycin ointment.
items.Caveat: Product in workbook is ophthalmic ointment; manual review/off-label note.

items.Source_File: Pasted text (2).txt — Thai epistaxis practice guideline 2567 / 2024
items.Recommendation_Extract: After anterior nasal packing, consider anti-staphylococcal antibiotics for 7–10 days; anterior packing generally kept ~3 days.
items.Patch_Use: Add oral antibiotic only as manual-review swap after packing.
items.Caveat: Guideline does not name a specific oral antibiotic.

items.Source_File: Pasted text (2).txt — Thai epistaxis practice guideline 2567 / 2024
items.Recommendation_Extract: Posterior packing requires admission, head elevation, oxygen, antibiotics, hydration/humidification; severe/persistent bleeding may need ligation/embolization.
items.Caveat: Not a routine OPD prescription row.
